findings.text: Use this route as part of secure sharing prep with clear review steps.

findings.message: Future-looking claim appears without concrete contract details (date/status/milestone).
findings.text: Track roadmap impact before committing process changes.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: 1) Read device and network basics (it’s short, and it prevents the biggest mistakes). 2) If it fits your threat model: load the site once, then go offline while processing (offline mode walkthrough). 3) Use the scrubber. 4) Open the output PDF and sanity‑check it (content + behavior). 5) Share using a channel that matches your situation (official newsroom tip channels, a trusted journalist org, or a lawyer).

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: It’s not because links are “bad”. It’s because v1 is built around a strict, high‑risk default: **safe and boring beats feature‑complete**.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: If you need a “keep links” mode later, we can add it as an explicit toggle with warnings — but v1 defaults to maximum safety.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: Write down who you are worried about. List what they can realistically access (device logs, network logs, account history). Choose the minimum safe workflow that addresses those risks. Practice once with a non-sensitive file.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: Treat anonymity as a full workflow, not a single tool. Clean document metadata before sharing. Avoid work devices and work networks when risk is high. Use official secure channels from trusted newsrooms or legal organizations.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: No single device setup is universally safe. Choose a workflow based on your actual risk and legal context.

findings.message: Future-looking claim appears without concrete contract details (date/status/milestone).
findings.text: v1 prioritizes preserving selectable text and core document structure. Full rasterization or advanced image cleaning is a future mode.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: Start with official secure submission instructions from the destination organization. If Tor is recommended, follow that organization's exact process. Test your workflow with non-sensitive files first.

findings.message: Trust/safety claim appears without mechanism or explicit limitation in the same paragraph.
findings.text: This default prioritizes safety over feature retention. Future modes may allow controlled toggles with warnings.

findings.message: Jargon appears in non-technical context without a quick plain-language definition.
findings.text: Not guaranteed in v1. EXIF may remain in embedded images, so high-risk cases should treat image metadata as potentially present.

findings.message: Jargon appears in non-technical context without a quick plain-language definition.

findings.message: Jargon appears in non-technical context without a quick plain-language definition.
findings.text: Watch for EXIF warnings in the scrub report. Prefer documents that do not depend on embedded photos when possible. If risk is serious, get legal or professional guidance before sharing. Keep your workflow simple so review steps are not skipped.

findings.message: Jargon appears in non-technical context without a quick plain-language definition.
findings.text: Never reuse personal accounts for sensitive submissions. Separate daily life devices from sensitive workflows when possible. Prefer short factual messages. Extra detail can create unique fingerprints.
